title: Acetosella ornata
taxonomy: Acetosella ornata is a member of the family Oxalidaceae, commonly known as the wood-sorrel family. This family consists of about 700 species of flowering plants, mostly native to the tropics and subtropics. Acetosella ornata is a small, flowering plant native to tropical Africa. Within the Oxalidaceae family, Acetosella ornata can be further classified as a genus within the Oxalidaceae. More specific classification details for Acetosella ornata are not readily available.
uses: Acetosella ornata is known to have various traditional medicinal uses. It is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant's leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. Additionally, Acetosella ornata is believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in treating infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Acetosella ornata for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Acetosella ornata, also known as the red clover or yellow clover, is considered safe for most people when consumed in moderation. However, excessive consumption may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as bloating, gas, and stomach discomfort in some individuals.
